questions.question: Differentiate between an Input-Restricted Deque and an Output-Restricted Deque. (2 marks)
expectedPoints: Input-Restricted Deque: Insertion allowed at only one end, deletion allowed at both ends.
Output-Restricted Deque: Deletion allowed from only one end, insertion allowed at both ends.

questions.question: Explain the implementation of a Priority Queue using an unsorted list and a sorted list. Compare their time complexities for insertion and deletion operations. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Unsorted List: Insertion is O(1) (add at end), Deletion is O(n) (linear search to find highest priority element).
Sorted List: Insertion is O(n) (find correct position to maintain sorted order), Deletion is O(1) (remove from end or beginning).
Comparison: Unsorted is good for frequent inserts, Sorted is good for frequent deletions.
Mention that a heap offers a better balance with O(log n) for both.
Provide an example scenario for each (e.g., unsorted for a logging system, sorted for a high-frequency task dispatcher).
